goal: Define the mock-only auth header contract before the local HTTP mock route gets any auth-gate implementation.
claims: BP-017 defines a mock-only auth header contract.
The contract forbids real tokens, cookies, secrets, login, user identity, and productive auth claims.
The contract defines allowed header name, local value, blocked responses, and tests for the implementation task.
assumptions: Auth is a batching boundary, so implementation must wait for a separate contract-backed task.
The current mock route remains reviewable before mock-auth implementation.
No runtime, package, tool, example, or existing docs edit is required.
dod: The document defines header name, allowed value, missing/wrong behavior, tests, non-goals, and BP-018 implementation boundary.
stop_conditions: Real token, secret, cookie, login, persistence, or live Builder behavior becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-016 added cohesive block-run rules but explicitly keeps Auth as a boundary. BP-015 requires a mock-auth-header contract before implementation.
